methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Ranovus is a Series D+ semiconductor company founded by Hamid Arabzadeh that has raised $236M to date, including a $36M strategic investment fund grant in August 2025. The valuation universe consists of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 10 same-sector peers, no meaningful peer set emerged after applying stage, adjacency, and valuation-tier filters. The data required a minimum of three datapoints within narrow cohorts (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies); zero qualified on each criterion. Relying on an all-sector median would conflate companies with materially different risk profiles, capital intensity, and time-to-revenue, rendering the result unreliable for a deep-tech semiconductor company at Series D+ stage.

Without sufficient narrow peer density to support triangulation, this valuation lacks a defensible quantitative anchor. The analysis would require either: additional same-stage or near-stage private comparable financials, or public semiconductor multiples applied to Ranovus-specific financial metrics (neither provided in available data). The $236M cumulative capital raised establishes a floor for historical valuation but does not support a forward valuation range. Material uncertainties include product commercialization timeline, addressable market traction, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set.
